channel.title: Hey, I'm Andreas Saks on My New Hugo Site
channel.description: Recent content in Hey, I'm Andreas Saks on My New Hugo Site

item.title: Homelab & Selfhosting
item.description:
I have a Synology NAS and two Mini-PCs in my homelab and a VPS I rent at netcup (I have rented several servers from them since around 10 years now, can highly recommend them)

In my homelab I run Proxmox on both of the Mini-PCs. I considered running Kubernetes on them, but I like actually using the software on it instead of just debugging the setup all the time. So I prefer Proxmox for it’s simplicity.

item.title: Tools and other stuff
item.description:
PC

  • OS: Mainly Nobara + Windows 11 for Adobe stuff and some rhythm games
  • CPU: Ryzen 7 5700X3D
  • GPU: Radeon™ RX 7800 XT
  • RAM: idk know which one exactly, but 24GB (2x8GB + 2x4GB)
  • MOBO: Gigabyte B450M DS3H

PC Accessories etc.

  • Audiointerface: Focusrite 2i2
  • Speakers: Logitech Z333 (the ones with the fake tweeters)
  • Laptop: Thinkpad E14

Homelab

  • NAS: Synology DS923+
  • Mini-PCs running in a Proxmox cluster
    • HP Prodesk 600 g6
    • Optiplex 7040 (idk what the specs are, it is my secondary machine mainly used for testing backups)

more details

item.title: About Me
item.description:
Hello there!

I’m a fullstack software developer currently working for LVM Versicherungen. I am mostly doing backend work, but also occasionaly frontend.

When I’m not coding, you can find me working on my homelab, gaming, looking like a dumbass while making photos of random things on the street, or bouldering.

One of my niche hobbies are probably arcade rhythm games. I played them a lot of them on my trips to Japan, but I also love visiting arcades in Europe. They are really rare around here, but the people running and visting them are usually way more passionate and dedicated, which I love.

item.title: Portfolio
item.description: These are some of the projects I’ve worked on, ranging from large-scale professional applications to personal experiments in code.
